---
title: "Day 2 — Everyday AI-Assisted Coding"
toc: true
toc-location: right
---

**Session goals:**

- Address questions and blockers from the self-study period
- Learn to plan and execute complex, multi-step tasks with an AI agent
- Understand prompt engineering, token cost, and when to stop using AI
- Get hands-on with advanced use cases: agentic development, Positron Data Bot

By the end of this session you should be able to delegate a complex analytical task to an
AI agent, supervise its execution, and course-correct when it goes off track.

---

## Session Structure (3 hours)

| Block | Duration | Topic |
|-------|----------|-------|
| 1 | 30 min | Q&A from self-study — open discussion of challenges and discoveries |
| 2 | 20 min | Demo: planning and executing a complex task with an agent |
| 3 | 40 min | [Advanced topics presentation](advanced-topics.qmd) |
| 4 | 60 min | [Case studies](case-studies.qmd) — hands-on practice |
| 5 | 10 min | Q&A and wrap-up |

---

## Course Completion

To complete the course, submit **two deliverables**:

| Deliverable | When | What |
|-------------|------|------|
| Self-study case study | Before Day 2 | See [Self-Study](../self-study/index.qmd) |
| Day 2 case study | Within one week after Day 2 | See [Day 2 Case Studies](case-studies.qmd) |

Both submissions demonstrate independent use of AI assistance for coding.
Successful submission is required for course completion.
